channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: [UN: March 13] Massive Upheaval in Lebanon, US War on Global Women’s Rights & Lots More
item.description: This Week @UN: Haitians stuck in Mexico; seeking the right UN SecGen; US battles women’s rights; Security Council condemns Iran; triumphing over sexual harassment. Plus: Mass displacement in Lebanon; SecGen in Türkiye & Lebanon; Gaza/West Bank/Israel; drone deadfare in Sudan; Strait of Hormuz strangled. Which country has the longest continuous coastline? Did someone forward this ... Read more

item.title: IDB to connect Latin America critical minerals supply with global demand
item.description: The Inter-American Development Bank launched a new initiative to ensure that both Latin America and the world can benefit from the region's mineral wealth.

item.title: Is a transcontinental highway the key to unlocking trade in Latin America?
item.description: IDB's new South Connection program sets the stage for massive transcontinental infrastructure projects, harmonized regulations, and digitalized customs and trade to ease regional logistics. Will it work?

item.title: Beyond the battlefield: The global ripple effects of the Iran war
item.description: From displacing millions in the Middle East to driving up food and fuel prices in Africa and threatening fragile peace efforts in Sudan, the war’s shockwaves are being felt worldwide.

item.title: UN Launches 300 Million Dollar Humanitarian Appeal for Lebanon
item.description: During a solidarity visit to Lebanon, the UN chief announced a flash appeal of USD 308.3 million to support humanitarian operations there in the wake of escalated fighting. The humanitarian appeal is intended to reach the more than 816,000 people within Lebanon that have been displaced due to the most recent fighting in the Middle […]

item.title: Who were IDB's top contractors of 2024?
item.description: IDB financed $2.3 billion worth of active contracts in 2024. Where did the money go, and who got the largest share? Devex analyzed the numbers to find out.

item.title: Devex Career Hub: How recruitment has shifted at UNICEF
item.description: This week: Key findings from our recent job board data analysis of UNICEF postings; why a former USAID staffer pivoted back to entrepreneurship; featured new job leads; and more.

item.title: The top global development employers in Indonesia
item.description: Devex rounds up the U.N. agencies and other global development employers that posted the most jobs across Indonesia in 2025.

item.title: Special edition: A tale of two movements — all at the same time
item.description: At the Commission on the Status of Women, the tangle over money, policy, and influence is shaping the future of women’s rights.

item.title: War in the Middle East will impact neglected disease R&D globally
item.description: Conflict-driven oil price surges from the Iran war will raise biomedical R&D costs, threatening to translate directly into reduced research activity. This shock severely undermines the already fragile, underfunded global R&D pipeline for neglected diseases.

item.title: From a brothel to the UN — is the aid sector ready for survivor leadership?
item.description: Opinion: The aid sector celebrates survivor stories but rarely funds survivor leadership. A leader raised in Mumbai’s red-light area argues that real localization means trusting communities to lead.

item.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 13 March: Displacement and civilian casualties mount across region
item.description: Escalating hostilities across the Middle East continue to drive civilian casualties, mass displacement and mounting humanitarian needs. Fighting along the Israel-Lebanon border has intensified, while millions across the region face growing risks from disrupted health services, overcrowded shelters and strained aid operations. UN agencies warn that children and vulnerable families are bearing the brunt as the conflict deepens and regional stability remains under severe pressure. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.

item.title: Middle East war: Women in Lebanon forced to give birth on roadside
item.description: As the UN Secretary-General touched down in Beirut on Friday in solidarity with the people of Lebanon, UN agencies highlighted the dangers for civilians and particularly pregnant women and migrant workers, amid ongoing airstrikes and rocket fire between Hezbollah fighters and Israel.

item.title: Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert
item.description: More than five years after Myanmar’s military coup, international resolve to hold the junta accountable must not weaken, an independent human rights expert warned on Friday, as escalating violence and growing humanitarian needs push millions of civilians deeper into crisis.

item.title: UN chief launches major humanitarian appeal from war-torn Lebanon
item.description: In a solidarity visit to the war-ravaged capital of Lebanon, the UN chief on Friday announced a flash humanitarian appeal of $308.3 million to support civilians there, after ongoing Israeli strikes countering rocket launches from Hezbollah left around 816,000 Lebanese internally displaced, a figure that looks set to rise.

item.title: Devex Newswire: Kenya braces for life after billions in US aid
item.description: Between 2020 and 2025, USAID committed roughly $2.5 billion to Kenya. Now that aid has shrunk and the strain is showing up in counties, clinics, and households. Plus, the latest from the IDB meetings and CSW conference.

item.title: As aid dries up, Plan International accelerates support for women
item.description: Devex speaks with Reena Ghelani, Plan International's chief executive officer, about how the organization is adapting to a more challenging world for women and girls.

item.title: Syria’s Mobile Cultural Bus: Championing Cultural Justice, Delivering Art and Literature to Children of War
item.description: In the Al-Azraq camp in northern Syria, 10-year-old Abeer Al-Qaddour sits, browsing a colourful book with intense focus and curiosity. Nearby stands a bus, elegantly inscribed with the words ‘The Cultural Bus’. Around the vehicle, dozens of children have gathered with visible joy, engaging in collective drawing activities for the very first time. Not far […]

item.title: The figures that matter: Redefining capital in development finance
item.description: Opinion: Community commitment is development's most potent, undervalued force. Effective financing must shift to build upon the contributions and expertise of those closest to the challenges we aim to address.

item.title: Why Does African Leadership Lack Coordination on Reparations?
item.description: Professor Jude Osakwe—a Nigerian scholar at the Namibian University of Science and Technology (NUST) and Continental Chairman of the Nigerians in Diaspora Organisation Africa (NIDOAF)—has reiterated the absolute truth over Reparations for Africa, noting that African governments have consistently expressed only ’emotional solidarity’ over Reparations instead of tackling and addressing, with seriousness, this pertinent issue […]

item.title: Only 1 in 7 Countries is Led by a Woman– as Global Political Power Remains Dominated by Men
item.description: Across the world, women remain vastly under-represented in political leadership, with the most powerful decisions still overwhelmingly made by men. In 2026, only 28 countries are led by a woman Head of State or Government, while 101 countries have never had a woman leader, according to the latest data released by Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU) and UN Women. When […]

item.title: When the UN Fails Its Own: A Survivor’s Story
item.description: Although the United Nations is increasingly attacked for being ineffective on the world stage, some of its biggest failures are internal, especially when it fails to protect its own staff from sexual harassment in the workplace. Sexual harassment is not only a violation of the survivor’s physical integrity and human dignity, it is also a ... Read more
